task_completion.description: The original image is opened in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The original portrait-oriented image (a flower, 600x800) is visible in the main canvas of an image editing application (e.g., GIMP).

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the rotation tool or menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu containing rotation options (e.g., 'Image' -> 'Transform' in GIMP) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the option for a 90-degree clockwise rotation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Rotate 90° clockwise' (or equivalent) option is highlighted or clicked in the menu.

task_completion.description: The image is successfully rotated on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the editor is now in landscape orientation (800x600), having been rotated 90 degrees to the right.

task_completion.description: The agent saves or exports the rotated image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog is visible (often with the filename 'flower_rotated_90cw.jpg'), or the agent clicks a save/export button to write the changes to disk.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the image editor's GUI (menus, dialogs) rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine image editing application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to an image editor (e.g., GIMP) and not a text editor or a script execution.

integrity.description: The agent performed the rotation rather than opening an already rotated image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the transition of the image from portrait to landscape within the editor's workspace.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is a 600x800 portrait photo of a flower. A correct 90-degree clockwise rotation will swap its dimensions to 800x600 (landscape). In GIMP, the standard path is Image -> Transform -> Rotate 90° clockwise. The expected output filename is typically 'flower_rotated_90cw.jpg'.
